title: Capacity, Observability, Support, and Production Operations · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for capacity, observability, support, and production operations and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for operational readiness and capacity case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty.
Measure task availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, and support from the user boundary and correlate them with system signals.
Design logs, metrics, traces, profiles, audits, retention, access, redaction, sampling, cardinality, and cost around diagnostic questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Capacity Model · changed case · Quiz Capacity Model
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The quiz variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Capacity Model without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Capacity Model · changed case · Quiz Capacity Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model demand, concurrency, data, compute, memory, storage, network, third-party quotas, growth, bursts, and failure reserve with uncertainty. For Capacity Model · changed case · Quiz Capacity Model, keep Capacity Model connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Incident Game Day
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Incident Game Day: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Feature completion hides an unrealistic workload, system-centered metrics, inaccessible support, untested runbook, and no compound incident rehearsal. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Incident Game Day.
steps.why: Retrieving Incident Game Day through the concrete operational readiness and capacity case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Incident Game Day, keep Incident Game Day connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject a realistic compound failure, observe detection, triage, communication, containment, recovery, reconciliation, and follow-up under time pressure.
